2605.19738.title: TERGAD: Structure-Aware Text-Enhanced Representations for Graph Anomaly Detection
2605.19738.paper_summary: Graph Anomaly Detection (GAD) aims to identify atypical graph entities, such as nodes, edges, or substructures, that deviate significantly from the majority. While existing text-rich approaches typically integrate structural context into the data representation pipeline using raw textual features, they often neglect the structural context of nodes. This limitation hinders their ability to detect sophisticated anomalies arising from inconsistencies between a node's inherent content and its topological role. To bridge this gap, we propose TERGAD (Structure-aware Text-enhanced Representations for Graph Anomaly Detection), A novel data augmentation framework that enriches structural semantics for GAD via the semantic reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). Specifically, TERGAD translates node-level topological properties into descriptive natural language narratives, which are subsequently processed by an LLM to derive high-level semantic embeddings. These embeddings are then adaptively fused with original node attributes through a gated dual-branch autoencoder to jointly reconstruct both graph structure and node features. The anomaly score is computed based on the integrated reconstruction error, effectively capturing deviations in both observable attributes and LLM-informed semantic expectations. Extensive experiments on six real-world datasets demonstrate that TERGAD consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines. Furthermore, our ablation studies validate the indispensable role of structural semantic guidance and the efficacy of the gated fusion mechanism. Code is available at https://github.com/Kantorakitty/TERGAD-main.
2605.19738.paper_summary_zh: 圖形異常檢測（GAD）旨在識別與大多數顯著偏離的非典型圖形實體，例如節點、邊或子結構。雖然現有的文本豐富方法通常通過使用原始文本特徵將結構上下文整合到數據表示管道中，但它們往往忽視了節點的結構上下文。這一限制妨礙了它們檢測由節點固有內容與其拓撲角色之間不一致性引起的複雜異常的能力。為了填補這一空白，我們提出了TERGAD（結構感知的文本增強表示用於圖形異常檢測），這是一個新穎的數據增強框架，通過大型語言模型（LLMs）的語義推理能力來豐富GAD的結構語義。具體而言，TERGAD將節點級的拓撲特性轉換為描述性自然語言敘述，這些敘述隨後由LLM處理以推導出高層次的語義嵌入。然後，這些嵌入通過一個門控雙分支自編碼器與原始節點屬性自適應融合，以共同重建圖形結構和節點特徵。異常分數是基於整合的重建誤差計算的，有效捕捉可觀察屬性和LLM知情的語義期望之間的偏差。在六個真實世界數據集上的廣泛實驗表明，TERGAD始終優於最先進的基準。此外，我們的消融研究驗證了結構語義指導的不可或缺性和門控融合機制的有效性。代碼可在 https://github.com/Kantorakitty/TERGAD-main 獲得。
2605.19738.author: Wen Shi et.al.
2605.19738.authors: Wen Shi, Zhe Wang, Huafei Huang, Qing Qing, Ziqi Xu, Qixin Zhang, Xikun Zhang, Renqiang Luo, Feng Xia
